query_normalized: can publicly available data reveal subtle violations of time-reversal symmetry in beta decay? physics
rationale: The paper focuses on neutrinoless double beta decay and lepton number violation (Majorana neutrinos), which is a distinct physical mechanism from the time-reversal symmetry (T-symmetry) violation in standard beta decay (typically probed via T-odd correlations like the D-coefficient). While both topics fall under nuclear and particle physics, the paper does not measure the specific observable (T-violation) or address the specific mechanism (time-reversal symmetry breaking) requested by the user,
